# FANDOM WIKI INTEGRATION GUIDE — GK BRAIN

Full guide to how GK BRAIN writes content to `gkniftyheads.fandom.com`.

---

## 1. Overview

The Fandom wiki at `https://gkniftyheads.fandom.com` is the canonical public reference for
the Crypto Moonboys / GKniftyHEADS universe. GK BRAIN's Wiki Brain (`wiki-brain.py`) is
responsible for keeping it up to date with real-world facts discovered by the Crawl Brain.

**Critical rule:** Telegram lore posts generated by Brain 3 (`gk-brain.py`) are **NEVER**
written to the wiki (DB-1). Only content crawled from approved official sources may feed wiki updates.

---

## 2. Architecture

```
crawl-brain.py
    │  writes crawl-results.json
    ▼
gk-brain.py
    │  queues ONLY web-sourced facts to wiki-update-queue.json
    ▼
wiki-brain.py
    │  health check → login → smart-merge → page-build → cross-check
    ▼
gkniftyheads.fandom.com (MediaWiki API)
```

Supporting modules called internally by `wiki-brain.py`:
- `wiki-smart-merger.py` — source validation + wikitext merging
- `wiki-page-builder.py` — infobox + categories + wikilinks scaffolding
- `wiki-cross-checker.py` — consistency audit between pages
- `wiki-citation-checker.py` — verifies that all cited URLs are live (HTTP 2xx)

---

## 3. Authentication

Authentication uses MediaWiki `clientlogin` API via `fandom_auth.py`:

1. GET `logintoken` from `/api.php?action=query&meta=tokens&type=login`
2. POST to `clientlogin` with `FANDOM_BOT_USER` + `FANDOM_BOT_PASSWORD` + `logintoken`
3. On success, GET a `csrftoken` for all subsequent edit calls

If login fails, `wiki-brain.py` aborts **all writes** for that run (DB-7). No retries in the same cycle.

---

## 4. Source Validation Gate

Before any queue entry is written to the wiki, `_is_valid_wiki_source()` in
`wiki-smart-merger.py` checks:

1. Source is not `gk-brain-agent`
2. Source does not contain `"telegram"`
3. Update type is not in `{"lore-post", "telegram-lore", "brain-lore"}`
4. Source URL contains at least one keyword from `_OFFICIAL_SOURCE_KEYWORDS`

Approved source domains: `substack`, `medium`, `graffpunks.live`, `graffitikings.co.uk`,
`gkniftyheads.com`, `youtube`, `youtu.be`, `twitter.com`, `x.com`, `instagram.com`,
`facebook.com`, `linktr.ee`, `fandom.com`, `fandom.wiki`.

See `OFFICIAL-SOURCE-AUTHORITY-RULES.md` for the full policy and sync requirements.

---

## 5. Page Structure Rules

Every wiki page created by GK BRAIN must include:

- **Infobox** — correct template for entity type (Character, Location, Event, etc.)
- **Wikilinks** — every keyword matching an existing page title uses `[[Page Title]]` syntax
- **Categories** — entity-type category + faction category + `GK BRAIN Auto-Generated`
- **Image** — uploaded to wiki, embedded as `[[File:Name.ext|thumb|right|Caption.]]`; if missing, add `[[Category:Pages needing images]]`
- **References** — all data sourced from official URLs, cited with `<ref>` tags

---

## 6. Wookieepedia-Style Granularity

The wiki should be built atomically — one page per concept, however small. Examples:

- One page per faction (40 pages minimum)
- One page per character
- One page per key event (Triple Fork Event, Final Fork Prophecy, etc.)
- One page per technology (Echo Ink, Sacred Chain, Null-Cipher, AETHER CHAIN, etc.)
- One page per location
- One page per NFT collection

This "key-ring" approach means the wiki grows organically and is deeply cross-linked.

---

## 7. What NOT to Write to the Wiki

- Brain 3 Telegram lore posts (DB-1)
- Content from `genesis-lore.md` (internal creative seed)
- Fan-invented content from non-official sources (OFFICIAL-SOURCE-AUTHORITY-RULES.md Rule 6)
- Personal blogs or third-party speculation not verified against official sources

---

## 8. Dry Run

Test wiki writes without hitting the live API:

```bash
python wiki-brain.py --dry-run
```

Logs exactly what would be written, with source validation results.

---

## 9. Related Files

|---|---|
| `wiki-smart-merger.py` | Source guard + wikitext merge logic |
| `wiki-page-builder.py` | Infobox / category / wikilink scaffolding |
| `wiki-brain.py` | Orchestrator — health check, login, write loop |
| `wiki-citation-checker.py` | Live URL verification for existing citations |

| `wiki-gap-detector.py` | Detects pages that should exist but don't |
